title: Expose live vector indexing progress
description: Make vector-indexer status show the real execution pipeline: current repair file and batch, files completed, rate and ETA, remote embedding BUSY/IDLE and queue, last batch timing, fallback state, and explicitly labeled laptop CPU/memory.
acceptance_criteria: status.py reports collection freshness plus live repair progress and homelab service activity; runtime state is atomic and safe when absent/stale; service counters are authenticated; tests cover progress calculations and service state; repair/watcher are restarted and live output verified.
close_reason: Added live watcher queue state/status output, documented --show-queue, and fixed orphan deleted vs direct classification.

title: Add remote-first embeddings with local fallback
description: Implement a systemd-managed homelab embedding service and update vector-indexer so document embeddings prefer homelab, fall back to the existing CPU-limited local FastEmbed path, and continue writing to laptop-local Qdrant. Keep query embeddings local and preserve existing config compatibility.
design: Homelab runs a portable container under systemd. Laptop performs file discovery, chunking, metadata, and Qdrant writes. Remote and local use pinned intfloat/multilingual-e5-large. Remote failures open a five-minute cooldown before retry.
acceptance_criteria: Remote health and batch embedding API work; index.py and watcher.py use remote-first lazy fallback; local fallback keeps current resource limits; search.py and Qdrant remain local; existing Qdrant points survive backend failures; status reports remote health; tests cover remote, fallback, recovery, and failure safety.
notes: Post-deploy diagnosis: 30s embedding timeout misclassified long valid batches as outages, causing 5-minute local fallback and homelab idle periods. Fixed with 3s health preflight plus 300s accepted-request timeout; added regression test (5 total). A 32-chunk batch completed remotely in 21.1s. Commit 58fa45f fast-forward merged and pushed to main. Watcher and repair restarted on main.
close_reason: Remote-first embedding service deployed and verified; local fallback, status, tests, safety guard, snapshot, watcher, and repair are active. Code committed as 4cef2e0 and pushed to feature/remote-embeddings.

title: daily-check: fix 'q' key quitting app while typing in Metrics field
description: modeMetrics key handler intercepted 'q' as global quit without checking textInput focus, unlike modeDoneLog which guards editing state. Typing 'q' (e.g. 'q1.2') into Spoken Output metric closed the app instead of being entered as text. Fix: removed the quit case from modeMetrics; exit via Esc as before.
close_reason: Removed the q-quit intercept in modeMetrics; typing q into metric values now works. Rebuilt and installed the binary.

title: Fix silent-failure bugs found by audit (obsync, vector-indexer, ai_organizer, daily-check)
description: Fixes for bugs found in a 2026-07-02 audit triggered by two vault-bridge bugs (yt-dlp --print skip, hardcoded whisper --language en) — same pattern: exit 0 but real work silently didn't happen or happened wrong. See moonbase SYSTEM/AI-Sessions/2026-07-02-01-shell-go-silent-failure-audit.md for full findings. Fixed: vector-indexer/watcher.py SIGTERM handling, index.py silent per-file failures (now returns bool, tracked, exit 1), ai_organizer book_organizer.go/local_importer.go JSON parse error swallowing, ai_organizer.go batch failure tracking, library_auditor.go walk error tracking, daily-check writeDailyLines atomic write, daily-check fetchBeadsSummary skipped-repo surfacing. obsync.sh and qdrant-backup.sh and keepass_sync.sh fixed separately (~/.local/bin, not in this repo).
close_reason: Fixed and committed all 6 findings in this repo (watcher.py SIGTERM, index.py failure tracking, ai_organizer JSON-error propagation x2, ai_organizer.go batch tracking, library_auditor.go walk-error tracking, daily-check atomic write + skipped-repo surfacing). Verified: go build/vet/test pass for daily-check (119 tests), standalone go build for each ai_organizer .go file, python3 -m py_compile for both vector-indexer files. obsync.sh/qdrant-backup.sh/keepass_sync.sh fixed separately in ~/.local/bin (not tracked here).

title: daily-check comment mode should allow Enter to add first comment
description: User opens comment mode from a daily note item and expects Enter to start editing even when the item has no existing comment lines. Today Enter is a no-op unless a comment line already exists, while add is hidden behind 'a'. Update comment-mode behavior so Enter can start the first comment, and cover it with tests.
notes: Fixed comment mode UX: Enter now starts a new comment when the selected task has no existing comment lines, matching user expectation in daily notes. Added regression test for empty comment block and rebuilt ~/.local/bin/daily-check.
close_reason: Enter now starts comment entry for empty daily-note comment blocks; regression test added; live binary rebuilt.

title: TUI tree view in details pane
